mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and drive 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in prolonged rate persistence. The mechanism operates continuously when both conditions hold.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% near threshold, CPIAUCSL at 322.132 elevated
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 55% of 20-day windows when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration. This triggers flight-to-safety demand for Treasuries, driving 10-year yields downward as investors reduce equity exposure and increase duration holdings.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2%, baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of 20-day windows following UNRATE spikes > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening consumer demand and aggregate demand contraction. This reduces inflation expectations and causes the Fed to signal future rate cuts, driving 10-year Treasury yields downward as the market reprices terminal rate expectations lower.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 60-day windows when RSAFS growth falls below 2% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 104.5 (index 2017=100) while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows faster than 150k per month, indicating robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This reduces inflation pressures and allows the Fed to cut rates, driving 10-year Treasury yields downward as the market reprices growth-adjusted rate expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.9867, PAYEMS at 159539 thousand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of 20-day windows when both supply conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1200 thousand units annualized, indicating a sharp contraction in residential investment demand. This signals broader economic weakness, reduces inflation expectations, and prompts the Fed to ease policy, driving 10-year Treasury yields downward as the market prices in future rate cuts and lower terminal rates.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1321.0 thousand units, above threshold
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 5-day windows when HOUST falls below 1200k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains at or above 4.25% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized, creating a restrictive policy stance relative to growth. This combination signals the Fed is holding rates too high relative to economic fundamentals, increasing recession risk and driving 10-year Treasury yields downward as investors demand safety and lower rates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33%, GDPC1 at 23685.287 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with slowing growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of 60-day windows when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200k per month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains below 4.0%, indicating a tight labor market with strong wage pressure. This forces the Fed to maintain elevated rates to combat inflation, driving 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in persistent monetary tightness and higher terminal rates.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159539 thousand, UNRATE at 4.2%
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation persistence
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 55% of 20-day windows when both labor tightness conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines for two consecutive months while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 322, indicating stagflationary pressure. The Fed faces a policy dilemma: cutting rates risks higher inflation, but maintaining rates risks recession. This uncertainty drives 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market demands a risk premium for policy confusion and economic instability.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines 2 consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL > 322
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 60% of 5-day windows when both stagflation conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 12-month period.
